rationale: Re-verified at 5435f51a. Series-coherence / gate-forwardness lens holds; the fix round did NOT blur the 110/111 boundary. (1) The added third-defense AC (line 92) pins an EXISTING guard, not a new writer: gate.go:257 already reads `f.Res.Approves >= threshold && threshold > 0`, so the AC constructs a Panel{ExpectedReviewers:1, absent field} -> resolved threshold 0 -> Block, testable standalone against today's code. R7/R8's landable surface (recorded field + ApproveThreshold() interpreter + ReviewerCountNote) still needs no panel.json writer; the writer stays deferred to spec 110 (Scope Out unchanged). (2) Inert-until-consumed is now explicit and honest: R4 pins every loop: key INERT until a 110+ consumer; R9 makes config show annotate models:/loop:/runner: as 'declared, not yet enforced' — series sequencing reads truthfully. (3) go list -deps ./internal/panel imports 0 of internal/config (re-checked) — leaf stays config-free. The added test surface (populated round-trip, reviewer-floor refusals count>=1/sum>=2, third-defense pin, ADR content-anchor grep) maps onto the same natural 4-bead split (ADR / config / panel / cmd), still within 3-5 one-commit beads. R3's config-side reviewer floor and R7's record-side fallback plus the gate-side guard form the three deliberately-redundant threshold-skip defenses, all now pinned by AC. Approval holds.
findings: ADDRESSED (verify): the round-1 REQUEST_CHANGES asks I was asked to re-check land cleanly — R3.2's third-defense pin cites the real gate.go:257 `threshold > 0` clause (verified present) and the R8 text warns future editors not to delete it; R3.4's inert annotations appear in both R4 and R9; the honesty of the models:/loop:/runner: not-yet-enforced framing is preserved.

ADVISORY (unchanged from round 1, non-blocking): Goal 2 line 15 still says the panel: block 'is wired into the panel machinery as the creation-time default for a fresh panel.json' — read literally this reads as a 109 writer deliverable, whereas R6 / Scope In / Scope Out / Open Questions all correctly defer the writer to spec 110. Every authoritative section is crisp; only the Goal summary overstates. Cosmetic, does not gate approval.

ADVISORY (plan-time, unchanged): the two workflow beads (panel R7/R8, cmd R9) share target dir .mindspec/domains/workflow/*.md for doc-sync; the plan should give them distinct workflow doc files or a stable merge order to avoid same-file churn, and 109's workflow-doc edits may rebase-overlap in-flight spec 108's — a doc conflict, not a code conflict (source packages stay disjoint).

VERIFIED at 5435f51a: gate.go:257 threshold>0 guard exists as pinned; ApproveThreshold() remains the single home (panel.go:76); internal/panel imports 0 of internal/config; the third-defense AC needs no panel.json writer, keeping the 109/110 boundary intact.
